revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image for a game-development pixel-art terrain tile asset.

STRICT OUTPUT CONTRACT:
- One single PNG image only.
- Final sheet is exactly 64x64 pixels conceptually: 4 columns x 4 rows, 16 tiles total, each tile exactly 16x16 px.
- No visible grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, text, watermark, UI, or frame dividers.
- Fully opaque. Use solid #FF00FF magenta only for any empty/background space, but each terrain tile should fill its 16x16 cell edge to edge.
- Crisp 16-bit SNES-style pixel art: low-resolution pixel clusters, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, dark one-pixel outline where useful, top-left warm light source.
- Keep every frame inside its own 16x16 cell at the same scale.

PROFILE / PRESET INTENT:
Profile: snes16_platformer_v1. Preset: world_coastline_16, but resolve the subject as a mythical-Japan satoyama terrain autotile set for a mossy stone retaining wall used in terraced rice-paddy and shrine-approach maps.

ART DIRECTION:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, cheerful golden-hour warmth, hand-painted classic Japanese fantasy animation feel without copying any named franchise. Top-down game tile readability, no isometric angle, no vanishing point. Broad geographic/adventure-map readability rather than close-up realistic detail.

SUBJECT:
A mossy stone retaining wall autotile, 苔むした石積み擁壁, with raised grassy plateau above and lower grass below. The wall tiles must clearly show a three-layer height stack compressed into each 16x16 tile:
A) Coping cap / 笠石 near the upper edge: pale grey-green flat dressed capstones, subtle bevel highlight, bright grass visible on the high terrace behind them.
B) Vertical stone face through the middle: rounded fieldstones / 玉石 nozura-zumi, warm grey and grey-brown stones, bright upper curves, dark crevice shadows below each cobble, moss and tiny fern hints in joints, lower stones darker and greener. It must read as a forward-facing vertical wall face, not as a flat top-down rock texture.
C) Base shadow near the lower edge: warm dark contact shadow on lower grass anchoring the wall.
Organic irregularity: uneven capstone band, varied stone sizes, small wild plants in cracks, cohesive scale.

4x4 TILE LAYOUT:
Row 1, tiles 1-4: center high-plateau grass surface matching a bright mossy satoyama grass hue, broad subtle color drift and low-frequency texture, no wall visible. Include usable center/variant plateau tiles.
Row 2, tiles 1-4: edge wall tiles for N, S, W, E. Each edge carries the full three-layer stack in orientation-appropriate miniature form; S-edge is the primary forward-facing wall tile with the clearest capstone, vertical cobble face, and base shadow.
Row 3, tiles 1-4: outer corners NW, NE, SW, SE. Corners must be organically curved: the capstone band bends in a gentle arc, fieldstones turn naturally, base shadow wraps around. Absolutely no sharp 90-degree masonry corner.
Row 4, tiles 1-4: accent variants: mossy cracked wall section with a small fern; tiny stone-set drainage outlet; capstone with a fallen small flower; lower-wall section with thick lichen.

TILEMAP QUALITY:
Seamlessly tileable as a terrain autotile set. Avoid checkerboard/stamp repetition. Vary stone shapes, moss, edge breakup, lighting, and grass texture while keeping all tiles cohesive. Transitions should blend grass and stone naturally without hard rectangular bands.

NEGATIVE CONSTRAINTS:
No characters, creatures, birds, companion animals, samurai armor, kabuto, ninja outfit, two-sword daisho, gun, modern objects, horror mood, dark gloomy palette, logos, letter emblems, watermarks, UI, text, or likenesses of Mario, Nintendo, Zelda, Final Fantasy, Pokemon, Dragon Quest, Secret of Mana, Okami, Sakuna, or any other existing franchise.
